rxn_class_summary: Electrochemical azidohydroxylation and azidoazidation (diazidation) of α-trifluoromethyl alkenes with TMSN3 via a radical-polar crossover mechanism affording β-CF3 azido alcohols or 1,2-diazides.
electrochemical_role: Anodic single-electron oxidation of azide (from TMSN3) to azidyl radical; subsequent second anodic oxidation of the β-CF3 alkyl radical adduct to a benzylic/α-CF3 carbocation. Cathode reduces HOAc to H2 and acetate.
electron_flow: At the anode, TMSN3 is oxidized by SET to an azidyl radical, which adds to the electron-poor α-CF3 alkene to give a benzylic C-radical; a second anodic oxidation converts this radical to a stabilized α-CF3 benzylic cation, which is trapped by H2O (path 1) to give the azido alcohol or by TMSN3/azide (path 2) to give the 1,2-diazide. The cathode reduces HOAc to H2 and acetate.

probe_reagents.name: 1,1-diphenylethylene (DPE)
probe_reagents.observation: Trapping adduct 3a' detected by HRMS, supporting involvement of an azidyl/C-radical.

probe_reagents.name: methanol (as solvent)
probe_reagents.observation: Azidomethoxylation product 4i obtained in 42% yield, supporting a carbocation intermediate trapped by MeOH.

probe_reagents.name: TMSN3 (CV)
probe_reagents.observation: Oxidation peak at +0.93 V vs Ag+/Ag, lower than alkene 1a (+2.10 V), indicating TMSN3 is preferentially oxidized at the anode.

probe_reagents.name: trifluoromethyl alkene 1a (CV)
probe_reagents.observation: Oxidation peak at +2.10 V vs Ag+/Ag, higher than TMSN3, ruling out direct alkene oxidation as initiation.

notes: Chemodivergence between azidohydroxylation (3) and diazidation (4) is controlled by electrode (Ni vs Pt cathode; graphite rod vs graphite felt anode) and solvent (DMSO/HOAc vs MeCN/HOAc); MeOH solvent diverts the cation to azidomethoxylation product, consistent with a radical-polar crossover via a carbocation rather than a closed-shell radical pathway.
